mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for corporate earnings, mechanically increasing present values and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 7.5 percent, labor market slack signals severe demand destruction and recessionary pressures, causing equity valuations to contract and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 7.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal spending and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business confidence, driving corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with an out-of-sample accuracy below 52 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to higher corporate earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases consistently precede negative 20-day forward returns during late-cycle expansions, reversing the predicted direction.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls signal robust economic activity and wage growth, supporting consumer spending and equity valuations to produce positive S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 5-day forward returns across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 235, accelerating inflation forces monetary authorities to tighten policy, raising discount rates and compressing equity multiples to generate negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 235 consistently coincide with positive 60-day forward returns, indicating inflation tolerance rather than tightening.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and real personal spending confirms broad-based economic expansion, reducing recession risk premiums and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 and RSAFS yields a negative coefficient in predictive regressions for 20-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals supply-side constraints and profit margin compression, triggering immediate risk-off flows and negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles produce positive 5-day forward returns in over 60 percent of observations.
